action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 23 crashed because test_new_model.py loaded 'checkpoints/baseline/model.pt' after chdir to /opt/habitat-lab-src, resolving to the wrong absolute path. After 22 eval submissions, nav_seek_success remains 0.0. The best result was iteration 11 (GPS-only proportional) with 82 safe steps and 0.6 nav_to_pos_success. All pathfinder hybrids performed worse because of black-box issues with ShortestPathFollower or custom pathfinder bugs. Analysis: The delta-angle trick for direct pathfinder steering was proven correct in debug_pf_live.py (distance reduced 1.81→1.19). What hurt previous evals was (1) checkpoint path bugs in test scripts causing false negatives, and (2) poor switching logic / follower black-box behavior. I will now use a clean direct-pathfinder branch (delta-angle to waypoint idx=1) when geo>2.5 or dist>3.0, and fall back to a tuned local proportional controller with a derivative damping term to reduce overshoot. This avoids ShortestPathFollower entirely. Decision: Rewrite model.py with direct pathfinder + local PD controller, keep predict.py unchanged (it already passes env), write a 2-episode local test using absolute checkpoint path, run train + local test, and submit eval if it succeeds.
